description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a broad, wedge-shaped metal blade. The handle is made of a medium-brown wood with visible grain, ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip and slightly flared at the base. The blade is metallic, with a matte, weathered finish and a single sharp edge, while the opposite side is flat and squared, suitable for hammering. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a traditional wedged fit. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or branding visible. This axe is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds for chopping wood or general utility tasks.
description: A utilitarian hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal blade. The design is functional, with a single sharp edge and no visible branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the blade's flat poll and handle curvature visible.
description_view_3: Rear edge-on view showing the thickness of the blade and the handle's attachment point.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, medium-brown color, oval cross-section, flared base, smooth but grippy surface.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll and a broad, slightly curved cutting edge; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fit, blade head fitted onto the handle with no visible fasteners.
